title: POLICY ON DRUG USAGE AND TESTING

sections.title: Definition of Drug
sections.content: For the purposes of this Policy, “Drug” is defined to mean “drugs may be abused” which are substances that are used primarily for their psychoactive effects and have a high potential for abuse and addiction. These substances can alter mood, perception, and consciousness, leading to feelings of euphoria, relaxation, stimulation, or altered sensory experiences. These Drugs include illicit drugs such as marijuana, synthetic cannabinoids (otherwise known as synthetic marijuana), opium (that is morphine, heroine, codeine, pentacozine), cocaine (COC), d-Amphetamine (AMP), benzodia-zepine (BZO); d-methamphetamine (MET), and any other psychotropic substance; as well as legal drugs like alcohol and prescription medications when misused.

sections.title: Policy and Procedures
sections.intro: This policy applies to all students admitted/matriculated into the Federal University of Technology, Ikot Abasi. It provides that:
list: I. Use, possession, sale, or being under the influence of illegal drugs, or illegally used prescription drugs, prescription or evidence of drug paraphernalia by any student of Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi (FUTIA) is strictly prohibited by the University.

ii. Possession, sale, or use of narcotic, mind-altering, or other illicit drugs, except for one's own prescription by a licensed Physician, (which must be fully disclosed to, known and approved by the Director of Medical Services), is prohibited by the University.
iii. Possession or use of prescription drugs prescribed to another individual, or the resale of one's own prescription or prescription drugs, is prohibited by the University.
iv. All students of Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi shall consent and present themselves for drug testing as provided for by this policy.

v. Students with negative test results shall be allowed to continue with his/her studies. The Head of Department will advise the individual, provide a copy of the result to him/her and places a copy in the student's file.
vi. Students with positive test results shall be treated as provided for in various relevant sections of this policy document.

subsections.subtitle: i. Routine/mandatory drug testing:
list: a. Urine, blood or tissue sample drug screen at the University-approved testing facility is required on (a) all newly admitted students to ascertain their drug-use status; (b) all students to qualify them to proceed for clinical activities/postings, teaching practice, SIWES, or its equivalent.
b. The Office of the Provost of School through the Dean Student Affairs (DSA), will give instructions to students who obtain a positive drug screen.

c. The substances to be screened may include, but are not limited to alcohol, amphetamines, barbiturates, benzodiazepines, cocaine metabolites, cannabis metabolites, methadone, methaqualone, opioids, phencyclidine and propoxyphene.

d. The results for such tests will become part of the permanent student record, which they have the right to review, and will not be shared with unauthorised persons or bodies without the student's permission.

e. If screening results come back positive for tested substances, a medical review for confirmation will be performed by the testing facility prior to informing the appropriate University authorities.
f. Students will be required to pay for routine drug screening, confirmatory test or re-test.

g. A Student who test positive on a “routine” drug test will be referred to a medical review committee who will further review the test results. The student will have the opportunity to discuss positive test results and provide any documentation for a legitimate medical explanation for the positive test result.

h. A student who fails to appear for routine drug testing will be given an opportunity to explain the failure to appear. If the student agrees to be tested, another collection will be scheduled for a date not later than ten (10) days from the day for the first schedule. If the student fails to appear for the second time, the failure to appear shall be treated as if a positive test result had occurred.
i. All questions and concerns about drug testing may be directed to the Director Medical Services through the Dean Students Affairs (DSA).

j. If, in the judgment of the Vice Chancellor, on the advice of the DSA and/or medical review committee, a student represents a clear and present danger to self or to others, the University may take immediate steps to remove the student from academic or other activities. They may also take additional steps that may be necessary to minimize hazards to the student or to others, including securing emergency professional assistance.

subsections.subtitle: ii. Cause for test:
subsections.intro: Drug testing of students after the initial routine drug screening clearance will be done upon the following:
list: a. Reasonable suspicion of substance or drug use,
b. The unauthorized use or possession of alcohol on campus
c. The use or possession of illicit drugs at any time.
d. When a student is caught in possession, use or distribution of any form of illegal items within or around the University environment.
e. Suspected membership of secret cult, or indulging in cult related activities, or being under investigation for alleged involvement in secret cult activities.

detailed_clauses.label: a) Reasonable suspicion
detailed_clauses.content: Reasonable suspicion of substance use may be based upon, but is not limited to any of the following criteria:
criteria: I. Direct observation by School, staff, students, residents or colleagues of drugs or alcohol use or possession and / or demonstration of physical symptoms of the influence of drugs or alcohol while within or neighbouring University environment, or engaged in University sanctioned or sponsored activities.

ii. When a student exhibits behavior, or a pattern of behavior, or whose physical condition, is consistent with the signs and symptoms of being under the influence of alcohol or drugs. These behaviors may be, but are not limited to: unsteady gait; odor of alcohol or illegal drugs on the breath or body; rapid, thick or slurring speech; aggressive or abusive language or behavior; disorientation or lethargy; nausea, vomiting, sweating; dilated or pinpoint pupils; poor relationships with fellow students; dirty appearance; altered timing and attendance to classes, examinations with poor academic performance.
iii. Arrest or conviction for a drug or alcohol related offense; identification as the focus of a criminal investigation into illicit drug use, possession or trafficking
iv. Evidence that a student has tampered with or caused a falsification of a drug or alcohol test

detailed_clauses.label: b)
detailed_clauses.content: If a “reasonable suspicion” exists that a student is under the influence of drugs or alcohol, the student shall be subject to immediate testing. If a student is unwilling to produce the requested sample, the student will be allowed 30 minutes to reconsider the decision. Students who refuse to take the test after the 30 minutes waiting period will be treated as if the test result was positive.

detailed_clauses.label: c)
detailed_clauses.content: When the determination to test for cause has been made, the student will be informed on where and how to obtain an observed urine, blood or other samples for drug screen or other requested tests.

detailed_clauses.label: d)
detailed_clauses.content: Reports of suspected substance use should be made to the Vice Chancellor through the Dean Students Affairs (DSA) and should document in writing the specific behavioral observations that form the basis for suspect of a violation and how the behavior is affecting the students. Whenever possible, corroborating statements from other Schools, staff, students, friends or neighbours should be obtained. This information will be shared with the Students Disciplinary Committee (SDC).

detailed_clauses.label: e)
detailed_clauses.content: A Student who test positive on a “for cause” drug test will be referred to a medical review committee who will further review the test results. The student will have the opportunity to discuss positive test results and provide any documentation for a legitimate medical explanation for the positive test result.

detailed_clauses.label: f)
detailed_clauses.content: The Students Disciplinary Committee (SDC) will review all information, meet with the student to determine whether the student has violated the policy based on a preponderance of evidence, and discuss consequences and potential academic or professional difficulties arising from suspected drug use. The SDC can vote to require subsequent and follow up drug testing.

detailed_clauses.label: g)
detailed_clauses.content: For cause testing, the costs will be borne by the University.

detailed_clauses.label: h)
detailed_clauses.content: If, in the judgment of the Vice Chancellor, on the advice of the DSA and/or medical review committee, a student represents a clear and present danger to self or to others, the University may take immediate steps to remove the student from academic or clinical activities. They may also take additional steps that may be necessary to minimize hazards to the student or to others, including securing emergency professional assistance.

subsections.subtitle: iii. Substance Use Self-Referral
subsections.intro: The University encourages students who have developed substance use problems to voluntarily identify themselves and to seek support and treatment.
list: a. When a student self-refers for treatment or counseling, no official report is made unless specific permission is granted by the student. Students who wish to receive assistance may be referred to a University-approved facility.
b. A student who voluntarily self-identifies as a substance user may be permitted to continue their current course of study without suspension, provided:
i. This self-identification occurs prior to any incident that is grounds for suspension or dismissal under institutional policy.
ii. The student immediately enters a treatment / rehabilitation program for the drug or alcohol abuse approved by the University.
iii. The student's conduct and academic performance meet the curricular expectations, and does not risk self or others.

c. If the self-identified student is due to commence, or already undergoing SIWES, elective, teaching practice, clinical postings or other equivalents courses, the student is required to seek or cause an approval for a temporary withdrawal from academic activities, in accordance with extant University rules and regulations, until treatment is complete and/or they can safely return, as determined by the Medical review committee.

d. A student who voluntarily self-identifies with a substance use problem and who, by his/her own admission, by the testimony of approved abuse counselors, or by the determination of institutional authority is no longer capable of acceptable academic and professional conduct, will be required to:
i. Seek or cause an approval for a deferment or a temporary withdrawal from academic activities, in accordance with extant University rules and regulations

ii. Proceed for medical rehabilitation, during the period of deferment or temporary withdrawal from studies, until treatment is complete and/or they can safely return, as determined by the Medical review committee.
e. A certified Medical Report should be provided by the students who self-refer.

f. Nothing in this policy will preclude the University from imposing a suspension, expulsion or taking other appropriate action against a self-referred student, for unacceptable academic performance or lack of appropriate conduct or behaviour.

sections.title: Drug Testing Laboratory
sections.content: Drug testing for students of Federal University of Technology Ikot Abasi shall be conducted by the University-approved certified laboratory within or outside the University.

sections.title: Medical Review of Positive Drug Test Results
list: i. All specimens identified as positive on the initial test shall be confirmed by the testing laboratory. Any positive test result will be reviewed by the Medical Review Committee.

ii. A Medical Review Committee (MRC), shall consist of academic staff from departments with degree-awarding programmes, and shall, among others, include a medical doctor, medical laboratory scientist, a psychiatric nurse and pharmacologist or toxicologist. The Director of Medical Services of the University shall be the chairman.
mrc_duties: i. Review and interpret positive test results.

ii. Examine alternate medical explanations for any positive test results. This may include conducting a medical interview and review of the student's medical history, or review of any other relevant biomedical factors.
iii. Review all medical records made available by the tested student when a confirmed positive test could have resulted from legally prescribed medication.

iv. Give the student an opportunity to discuss the results before making a final decision on the results of the confirmed positive test. In doing so, it shall contact the student directly to discuss the results of the test.

sections.title: Appeal and Retesting
sections.content: A student who has a positive test for drug usage may appeal by submitting a written request to the Vice Chancellor through the Dean Students Affairs (DSA) within five (5) working days after being informed of the positive test results. The student has the right to have a confirmatory test performed on the original specimen at any University-approved certified laboratory of his/her choice. All expenses of such retests will be the responsibility of the student. The specimen transfer between the laboratories will follow standard protocol.

sections.title: Penalties
sections.intro: Violations of this policy may result, subject to the due process rights to which a student is entitled, in disciplinary action, including suspension or expulsion from the University.
list: i. Newly admitted students who test positive for drug use will be advised to withdraw from academic activities and defer the admission for a period of one academic session and proceed for rehabilitation in the University's approved facility and at their own cost. Such withdrawal from studies for testing positive for drug use shall apply notwithstanding that the University conducts the test after registration. All academic activities done including payments made to the University shall be forfeited, except the student's admission.

ii. Any student who fails a mandatory or for cause drug test for the first time will be suspended for an academic session and proceed for rehabilitation in the University's approved facility and at his/her own cost. All University rules concerning suspension/deferment of studies shall apply.
iii. A student who violates this policy for a second time will be subject to disciplinary actions including expulsion/dismissal from the University according to approved University laws.

iv. For a student who failed a drug test, his/her participation in, and successful completion of an approved drug or alcohol counseling/rehabilitation program coupled with his/her consent to random testing and undertaking to be of good behaviour, may be considered to continue his/her programme of study. Such a Student is required to be negative for a drug re-test, after providing a satisfactory medical report certified and authenticated by the Director of Medical Services of the University.

v. Any student found responsible for possession with intent to use, sell or distribute illegal drugs on campus and/or off campus will be expelled from the University notwithstanding that such a student may be a first or second time offender.

vi. Refusal to Consent to Testing: If a student refuses to consent to an alcohol or drug test, the student will be treated as if the test result was positive, and will be subject to appropriate disciplinary actions, including recommendation for expulsion.

sections.title: Implementation of Policy
list: I. Conduct drug tests on all admitted students of this University to ascertain their drug-use status;
ii. Reserve the right to charge a fee for the drug test
iii. Communicate the results of the tests to students.
iv. Administer appropriate sanctions on students who violate this policy.

sections.title: Publication of policy
list: I. Publish this policy on the University website, especially on Admissions portal, Basic/Foundation Programmes portal, during events organised or sponsored by the University, as well as in the social, electronic and print media, in order to sensitize parents, guardians and students, and those applying for admissions into any of the University programmes, about the University Policy on drug use.
ii. Publish this policy in the student handbook for each program, and other appropriate University publications to ensure adequate notice and distribution.

sections.title: Confidentiality
sections.content: The University will take reasonable measures to protect the confidentiality of individual test results and the student's medical history, to the extent possible. Students should be aware that test results may be used for administrative hearings and court cases and may be sent to State and /or Federal agencies as required by applicable laws and regulations regarding the release of such information.
